sourceMovie.title: Man on the Moon
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Man on the Moon (1999) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Annie Hall
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Man on the Moon clicked for you, Annie Hall (1977) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: October Sky
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Man on the Moon clicked for you, October Sky (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Man on the Moon?
faq.answer: Try Annie Hall, October Sky, Broadway Danny Rose—each shares DNA with Man on the Moon while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Man on the Moon?
faq.answer: Man on the Moon (1999) is best described as Comedy, Drama, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Man on the Moon worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
